behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module boundary (dispatcher -> reviewer clients), not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network behavior is required; in-process reviewer stubs can detect wrong payload routing. LLM-DEP demotion proof: behavior text is plumbing-only — "sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack"; it does not assert model judgment quality. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because no assertion on what the model decides, only what is sent.
behaviors.test_description: Wire dispatcher with three in-process fake reviewer adapters, run one contract through dispatch, and assert each adapter received identical document bytes plus its role-specific instruction pack.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model output quality (identifying legal terms), and outcomes can vary across runs/prompts. MOCK-HIDE: a stubbed extractor would always return expected keys and hide real extraction misses. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because behavior covers one reviewer decision, not a multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real clause-extractor model on representative contracts and assert required term categories are extracted with acceptable recall/precision thresholds.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is handoff correctness across modules (config builder -> risk assessor client). REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real network/process needed; a fake assessor endpoint/client can validate payload content. IA-UA demotion proof: behavior text is explicitly transport/config plumbing — "configuration is passed correctly ... invocation" — not policy scoring quality. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because it does not ask whether flagged results are correct.
behaviors.test_description: Execute risk-assessor invocation through real service wiring with a fake assessor receiver and assert the exact serialized policy config is present and unmodified.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: assertion is about semantic judgment quality (including indirect/euphemistic language), which requires real model behavior. MOCK-HIDE: deterministic stubs cannot reveal missed euphemisms. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because this behavior evaluates one reviewer’s decision quality, not chained agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk-assessor model on a curated set containing explicit and euphemistic risky clauses, and assert it flags policy-violating language above target detection thresholds.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is inter-module payload handoff (orchestrator/config -> compliance client). REAL-INFRA: no OS/subprocess/real network behavior is required to catch wrong fields. IA-UA demotion proof: text says "are passed correctly ... invocation," which is plumbing-only and not compliance decision quality. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because correctness target is request construction, not model output.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke compliance checker through production wiring against a fake receiver and assert jurisdiction and regulatory profile fields are correctly mapped and transmitted.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: behavior requires correctness of AI compliance determination, not just call success. MOCK-HIDE: stubs would mask real false positives/negatives in regulatory interpretation. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because this is a single reviewer quality check, not multi-step agent pipeline behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the real compliance-checker model on contracts with known regulatory outcomes and assert pass/fail determinations meet defined accuracy targets.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior targets deterministic merge-rule logic for one component given two inputs. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP: no real infra or live model judgment required; conflict records can be injected directly. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected because text is scoped to "the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution" and can be fully validated at the merge function/object boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Call merge logic with synthetic conflicting reviewer findings for the same clause and assert output contains both assessments plus correct reviewer attribution.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP: this explicitly chains multiple reviewer calls plus merge, and includes end-to-end retention of findings across stages. LLM-DEP: three live reviewer outputs influence downstream collection/merge correctness. MOCK-HIDE: replacing reviewers with fixtures can hide loss/misalignment introduced by real model outputs and sequencing/parallel fan-in. Highest applicable level is Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run full pipeline on real contracts using live extractor/risk/compliance reviewers in parallel, then assert merged report contains all findings from each reviewer with no dropped items and correct attribution.
